step_by_step_thinking: Based on reviewing the spirometry curve, I can see that the forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV1) is approximately 50 liters while the forced vital capacity (FVC) is around 70 liters. The calculation for FEV1/FVC would then be 50/70 which equals roughly 71%.
raw_response:
Based on reviewing the spirometry curve, I can see that the forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV1) is approximately 50 liters while the forced vital capacity (FVC) is around 70 liters. The calculation for FEV1/FVC would then be 50/70 which equals roughly 71%. 

Therefore, my answer choice is B: 70-79%.

This interpretation assumes standard units; if the graph uses different measurement scales or units it may require conversion to achieve an accurate result. However given typical ranges this seems appropriate based on visual inspection alone. Let me know if you need any clarification!
